title: named handlers (handler h = {…})
manager: Named handlers — `handler h = {…}` — reusable hook bodies you define once and attach in multiple places, instead of copy-pasting an inline hook onto every transition that needs it. This is what turns hooking from per-edge boilerplate into composable, named behavior, and named handlers are what a machine can later require or declare as part of its public interface.
cs: `handler h = {…}` named, reusable hook bodies; attachable at multiple points; the basis for required/declared hooks (W7.6); §12/§14.
